title: विवाह मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विवाह मन्त्र संग्रह ॥
॥ वैदिक विवाह पद्धति ॥

॥ गणेश वन्दना ॥

ॐ श्री गणेशाय नमः।
सुमुखश्चैकदन्तश्च कपिलो गजकर्णकः।
लम्बोदरश्च विकटो विघ्ननाशो गणाधिपः॥

॥ संकल्प मन्त्र ॥

ॐ विष्णुर्विष्णुर्विष्णुः।
श्रीमद्भगवतो महापुरुषस्य विष्णोराज्ञया प्रवर्तमानस्य...
अमुकगोत्रस्य अमुकशर्मणः कन्यादानं / पाणिग्रहणं अहं करिष्ये।

॥ कन्यादान मन्त्र ॥

ॐ कन्यां कनकसम्पन्नां कनकाभरणैर्युताम्।
दास्यामि विष्णवे तुभ्यं ब्रह्मलोकजिगीषया॥

विश्वम्भरः सर्वभूतः साक्षिण्यः सर्वदेवताः।
इमां कन्यां प्रदास्यामि पितॄणां तारणाय च॥

(कन्या का पिता वर के हाथ में कन्या का हाथ देता है — जल, अक्षत, पुष्प, दूर्वा सहित)

॥ मंगलसूत्र बन्धन मन्त्र ॥

ॐ मांगल्यं तन्तुनानेन मम जीवनहेतुना।
कण्ठे बध्नामि शुभगे सा जीव शरदः शतम्॥

इस मंगलसूत्र से मैं तुम्हारे कण्ठ में बाँधता हूँ — सौ वर्ष तक जीवित रहो, सौभाग्यवती रहो।

॥ सिन्दूरदान मन्त्र ॥

ॐ सुमंगली प्रतरस्य भूयासम्
सुपुत्रिणी सुभगा।
सिन्दूरं दास्यामि तुभ्यं
सौभाग्यं वर्धतां सदा॥

॥ अग्नि स्थापना मन्त्र ॥

ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः। ॐ अग्नये नमः।

ॐ अग्निरिदं समिन्धे दीर्घायुत्वाय वर्चसे।
आयुष्मान्त्सं प्रजया सं पशुभिः सं रायस्पोषेण
सं सौभाग्येन॥

हे अग्नि देव! आप साक्षी बनें। दीर्घायु, तेज, सन्तान, धन-धान्य और सौभाग्य प्रदान करें।

॥ सप्तपदी मन्त्र (सात फेरे) ॥

प्रथम पदम् —
ॐ एकमिषे विष्णुस्त्वान्वेतु।
(अन्न के लिये — भगवान विष्णु तुम्हें ले चलें)
परस्पर अन्न-धान्य की कभी कमी न हो।

द्वितीय पदम् —
ॐ द्वे ऊर्जे विष्णुस्त्वान्वेतु।
(बल के लिये — विष्णु तुम्हारा अनुगमन करें)
परस्पर शारीरिक और मानसिक बल प्राप्त हो।

तृतीय पदम् —
ॐ त्रीणि व्रताय विष्णुस्त्वान्वेतु।
(व्रत-धर्म के लिये — विष्णु सहचरी बनें)
धर्म का पालन साथ मिलकर करें।

चतुर्थ पदम् —
ॐ चत्वारि मायोभवाय विष्णुस्त्वान्वेतु।
(सुख के लिये — विष्णु आनन्द दें)
गृहस्थ जीवन में सदा सुख और आनन्द रहे।

पञ्चम पदम् —
ॐ पञ्च पशुभ्यो विष्णुस्त्वान्वेतु।
(पशुधन-सम्पत्ति के लिये)
धन-सम्पत्ति और ऐश्वर्य की प्राप्ति हो।

षष्ठ पदम् —
ॐ षड् ऋतुभ्यो विष्णुस्त्वान्वेतु।
(ऋतुओं के लिये — विष्णु रक्षा करें)
सदा ऋतुचर्या और स्वास्थ्य बना रहे।

सप्तम पदम् —
ॐ सप्त सप्तभ्यो होत्राभ्यो विष्णुस्त्वान्वेतु।
(सप्त होत्र, मित्रता के लिये)
सखा भव! अब हम सात पद चले — मित्रता अटल हो गई।

सखा सप्तपदा भव। सखायौ सप्तपदा बभूव।
सख्यं ते गमेयम्। सख्यात् ते मायोषम्।
सख्यान्मे मायोष्ठाः॥

॥ ध्रुवदर्शन मन्त्र ॥

(वर वधू को ध्रुव तारा और अरुन्धती तारा दिखाता है)

ॐ ध्रुवमसि ध्रुवं त्वा पश्यामि ध्रुवेध मे।
ध्रुवां पोष्यां पतिं मे अरुन्धती देही॥

जैसे ध्रुव तारा अचल है, वैसे तुम्हारा सौभाग्य अचल रहे। अरुन्धती (वशिष्ठ पत्नी) के समान पतिव्रता और गृहस्थ धर्म में स्थिर रहो।

॥ आशीर्वाद मन्त्र ॥

ॐ सम्राज्ञी श्वशुरे भव, सम्राज्ञी श्वश्र्वां भव।
ननान्दरि सम्राज्ञी भव, सम्राज्ञी अधि देवृषु॥
(ऋग्वेद १०.८५.४६)

हे वधू! ससुर के घर में, सास के सामने, ननद और देवरों में — सर्वत्र साम्राज्ञी (गृहलक्ष्मी) बनो।

ॐ सह नौ भगः सह नौ यशः।
सह नौ भोजनं सह नौ ज्योतिः॥

॥ शान्ति पाठ ॥

ॐ सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै।
तेजस्विनावधीतमस्तु मा विद्विषावहै॥
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ इति श्री विवाह मन्त्र संग्रह सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vivaha Mantra Sangraha ||

|| Ganesha Vandana ||
Om Shri Ganeshaya Namah.

|| Kanyadana Mantra ||
Om Kanyam Kanaka-sampannam Kanaka-abharanair-yutam.
Dasyami Vishnave Tubhyam Brahma-loka-jigishaya.

|| Mangalasutra Bandhana ||
Om Mangalyam Tantuna-anena Mama Jivana-hetuna.
Kanthe Badhnami Shubhage Sa Jiva Sharadah Shatam.

|| Agni Sthapana ||
Om Agnir-idam Samindhe Dirghayutvaya Varchase.

|| Saptapadi Mantra (Sapta Phere) ||
1. Om Ekam-Ishe Vishnus-tva-anvetu.
2. Om Dve Urje Vishnus-tva-anvetu.
3. Om Trini Vrataya Vishnus-tva-anvetu.
4. Om Chatvari Mayo-bhavaya Vishnus-tva-anvetu.
5. Om Pancha Pashubhyo Vishnus-tva-anvetu.
6. Om Shad Ritubhyo Vishnus-tva-anvetu.
7. Om Sapta Saptabhyo Hotrabhyo Vishnus-tva-anvetu.

Sakha Saptapada Bhava. Sakhayau Saptapada Babhuva.

|| Dhruva Darshana ||
Om Dhruvam-asi Dhruvam Tva Pashyami.

|| Ashirvada ||
Om Samrajni Shvashure Bhava, Samrajni Shvashrvam Bhava.
(Rigveda 10.85.46)

|| Shanti Patha ||
Om Saha Navavatu. Saha Nau Bhunaktu.
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih.

|| Iti Shri Vivaha Mantra Sangraha Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: वैदिक विवाह मन्त्र संग्रह — गणेश वन्दना से शुभारम्भ। कन्यादान मन्त्र — पिता कन्या को विष्णु रूप वर को अर्पित करता है। मंगलसूत्र मन्त्र — सौ वर्ष दीर्घायु और सौभाग्य का बन्धन। अग्नि स्थापना — अग्नि देव साक्षी, दीर्घायु-तेज-सन्तान-सम्पत्ति प्रदान करें। सप्तपदी — सात फेरे: (१) अन्न (२) बल (३) धर्म (४) सुख (५) सम्पत्ति (६) ऋतु/स्वास्थ्य (७) सखा-मित्रता। सात पद चलकर मित्रता अटल। ध्रुव-अरुन्धती दर्शन — सौभाग्य अचल रहे। आशीर्वाद — ससुराल में गृहलक्ष्मी बनो (ऋग्वेद)। शान्ति पाठ — साथ रक्षा, साथ भोग, साथ वीर्य, तेजस्वी अध्ययन, परस्पर द्वेष न हो।
benefits: Complete collection of authentic Vedic wedding ceremony mantras
Includes Saptapadi (seven steps) — the most sacred part of Hindu marriage
Kanyadana, Mangalasutra, Sinduradana mantras with meaning for understanding the ritual
Agni is the divine witness — connects the couple to the eternal fire of dharma
Dhruva-Arundhati darshana ensures stability and fidelity in marriage
Understanding these mantras deepens the spiritual significance of the wedding
description: Vivaha Mantras — विवाह मन्त्र — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Agni. Attributed to Rig Veda, Grihya Sutras (Ashvalayana / Paraskara), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to include Saptapadi (seven steps) — the most sacred part of Hindu marriage as well as dhruva-arundhati darshana ensures stability and fidelity in marriage. Additionally, it is known to agni is the divine witness — connects the couple to the eternal fire of dharma. Comprising 20 verses, it can be completed in approximately 15 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vivaha Mantras in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vivaha Mantra Sangraha?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vivaha Mantra Sangraha (विवाह मन्त्र संग्रह) is the comprehensive collection of authentic Vedic Sanskrit mantras prescribed for the eight major rites of the traditional Hindu wedding ceremony, drawn from the Rig Veda 10.85 (the famous Surya-sukta which is the foundational vivaha-sukta) and the Grihya Sutras of Ashvalayana, Paraskara, Apastamba, Bodhayana and Gobhila — the Vedic ritual manuals that codified the wedding paddhati. The eight rites mantras-collection includes: (1) Ganesha vandana (invoking Ganesha for vighna-nivarana), (2) Sankalpa (the formal declaration of intent), (3) Kanyadana (the giving of the bride to Vishnu in the form of the groom), (4) Mangalsutra-bandhana (tying the auspicious thread), (5) Sindurdana (applying vermillion to the bride's hair-parting), (6) Agni-sthapana (kindling the sacred fire), (7) Saptapadi (the seven steps — the most binding rite), (8) Dhruva-darshana (showing the Pole Star), and the closing Ashirvada and Shanti Patha. (Source: Rig Veda 10.85; Grihya Sutras of Ashvalayana, Paraskara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Saptapadi (Seven Steps) and why is it the most sacred part of Hindu marriage?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saptapadi (सप्तपदी — Seven Steps) is the constitutionally and ritually most binding part of the Hindu wedding — both Hindu Marriage Act 1955 and the Smritis declare the marriage legally complete only at the seventh step. The bride and groom together take seven steps around the sacred fire (Agni) — each step accompanied by a specific Vedic mantra and a specific marital vow: (1) Ekam Ishe Vishnus tva-anvetu — for food and nourishment, may Vishnu lead you; (2) Dve Urje — for strength of body and mind; (3) Trini Vrataya — for righteous conduct (vrata-dharma) together; (4) Chatvari Mayobhavaya — for happiness in grihastha life; (5) Pancha Pashubhyo — for prosperity and progeny; (6) Shad Ritubhyo — for harmony with the seasons and good health; (7) Sapta Saptabhyo Hotrabhyo — for the seven hotras (sacrificial relations) and eternal friendship. The seventh step concludes with Sakha saptapada bhava — Be my sakha (friend) by these seven steps; sakhayau saptapada babhuva — We have become friends by these seven steps. (Source: Ashvalayana Grihya Sutra 1.7.19; Rig Veda 10.85.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kanyadana mantra and what does it mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kanyadana (कन्यादान — gift of the daughter) is the ritually elevated moment when the bride's father places her hand in the groom's hand, declaring her gifted to Vishnu in the form of the groom. The mantra is: Om Kanyam kanaka-sampannam kanaka-abharanair-yutam, dasyami Vishnave tubhyam Brahma-loka-jigishaya — I give this maiden, adorned with gold and golden ornaments, to you who are Vishnu, with the desire of attaining Brahma-loka. The accompanying second mantra invokes the entire universe as witness: Vishvambharah sarva-bhutah sakshinyah sarva-devatah, imam kanyam pradasyami pitrunam taranaya cha — All-supporting Vishnu, all beings, all deities — be my witness as I give this maiden, also for the deliverance of my ancestors (pitrus). The water (jala), akshata (rice), pushpa (flowers) and durva (sacred grass) are poured into the joined hands of the couple, sealing the dana ritually. The phrase Brahma-loka-jigishaya makes Kanyadana not a property-transfer but a punya-karma giving the bride's family entry into Brahma-loka. (Source: Paraskara Grihya Sutra 1.4; Vivaha Mantra Sangraha.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mangalsutra-bandhana mantra and the symbolism of the auspicious thread?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mangalsutra-bandhana (tying of the auspicious thread) is the iconic moment when the groom ties the mangalsutra around the bride's neck, pronouncing her his life-partner. The mantra is: Om Mangalyam tantuna-anena mama jivana-hetuna, kanthe badhnami shubhage sa jiva sharadah shatam — With this auspicious thread, which is the means of my life, I tie around your neck, O auspicious one; may you live for a hundred autumns (sa jiva sharadah shatam). The mangalsutra (also called thali in South India, mangalya in Sanskrit) traditionally consists of a yellow turmeric-dyed cotton thread (in earliest Vedic times) or a black-bead chain with a gold pendant (in modern times) — the black beads believed to absorb negative energy from the bride. The phrase mama jivana-hetuna (which is the means of my life) makes the wife the source of the husband's very life-force in the grihastha-ashrama. The 100-year wish (sharadah shatam) is the standard Vedic blessing for full life-span. (Source: Paraskara Grihya Sutra; Vivaha Mantra Sangraha.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhruva-Arundhati darshana ritual and its significance?
seoFaqs.answer: After the Saptapadi, on the wedding night, the groom takes the bride outside and shows her two specific stars in the night sky — Dhruva (the Pole Star, alpha Ursae Minoris) and Arundhati (the smaller companion star Alcor in the Mizar pair of Ursa Major / the Saptarshi-mandala). The mantra recited is: Om Dhruvam-asi Dhruvam tva pashyami, dhruvedha me — You are the immovable one, I see you as immovable, may my life with you be immovable; and Dhruvam poshyam patim me Arundhati dehi — Grant me, O Arundhati, an immovable nourishing husband. The Dhruva star, eternally fixed at the celestial pole around which all other stars rotate, symbolises the unchanging fidelity of the marriage bond. Arundhati was the wife of sage Vasishtha, the supreme exemplar of pati-vrata-dharma (wifely fidelity) — she is one of the very few women elevated to the Saptarshi mandala alongside her husband. The bride is asked to model her marriage on Vasishtha-Arundhati's eternal partnership. (Source: Paraskara Grihya Sutra 1.8.19-20; Vivaha Mantra Sangraha.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Agni the divine witness in the Hindu wedding?
seoFaqs.answer: Agni (the sacred fire) is the supreme witness (sakshi) of the Hindu wedding — every important rite (Saptapadi, oblations, Pradakshina) is performed around the kindled Agni in the central wedding-mandapa. The Agni-sthapana mantra is: Om Bhur-bhuvah Svah, Om Agnaye namah, Om Agnir-idam samindhe dirghayutvaya varchase, ayushman tsam prajaya sam pashubhih sam rayas-poshena sam saubhagyena — I kindle this Agni for long life, splendour, longevity, progeny, cattle, prosperity and good fortune. Agni is the divine witness for three reasons: (1) Agni is the messenger between humans and devas (deva-doota) — every offering placed in him is conveyed to all the devas, so the entire deva-mandala witnesses the marriage simultaneously, (2) Agni is the eternal flame — the marriage performed before him is sealed for life and beyond, (3) Agni is purifying — burning away any impurity in the intentions of the couple. The Saptapadi mantras that follow each address Vishnu, but the steps are taken around Agni — making the marriage simultaneously a Vaishnava-bhakti act and an Agni-sakshi-pratijna (vow before Agni). (Source: Rig Veda; Grihya Sutras; Vivaha Mantra Sangraha.)
